record.field: Zabbix is an enterprise-grade open-source platform for real-time monitoring of networks, servers, virtual machines, applications and cloud services.

Zabbix is an enterprise-grade open-source platform for real-time monitoring of networks, servers, virtual machines, applications and cloud services.

It collects metrics through native agents, SNMP, IPMI, JMX, scripts and agentless checks, then evaluates them against trigger expressions to detect problems and drive escalation-based alerting. Designed for everything from a single host to distributed, multi-site infrastructures, it scales with proxies and supports high availability. Its template-driven model lets teams onboard new devices and services consistently with minimal manual configuration.

Key features
  • Flexible data collection: native agents, SNMP traps, IPMI, JMX, HTTP and custom scripts
  • Trigger-based problem detection with anomaly/baseline functions and dependency handling
  • Escalation workflows and multi-channel notifications (email, webhooks, messaging, ticketing)
  • Reusable templates, auto-discovery and low-level discovery for dynamic environments
  • Customizable dashboards, maps, graphs and reporting
  • Distributed monitoring via proxies plus native high-availability for the server
Best for

IT and DevOps teams, MSPs and enterprises that need a single, fully self-hosted solution to monitor heterogeneous infrastructure end to end with granular alerting and long-term data retention.

services: postgres: image: postgres:16-alpine restart: unless-stopped environment: POSTGRES_DB: {{ postgres_db }} POSTGRES_USER: {{ postgres_user }} POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ postgres_password }} volumes: - ./zabbix_postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U {{ postgres_user }} -d {{ postgres_db }} interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' networks: - internal zabbix-java-gateway: image: zabbix/zabbix-java-gateway:alpine-7.4-latest restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_zabbix_java_gateway' networks: - internal zabbix-snmptraps: image: zabbix/zabbix-snmptraps:alpine-7.4-latest restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./zabbix_snmptraps:/var/lib/zabbix/snmptraps - ./zabbix_mibs:/var/lib/zabbix/mibs container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_zabbix_snmptraps' networks: - internal zabbix-server-pgsql: image: zabbix/zabbix-server-pgsql:alpine-7.4-latest restart: unless-stopped environment: DB_SERVER_HOST: postgres POSTGRES_USER: {{ postgres_user }} POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ postgres_password }} POSTGRES_DB: {{ postgres_db }} ZBX_ENABLE_SNMP_TRAPS: 'true' ZBX_JAVAGATEWAY: zabbix-java-gateway depends_on: postgres: condition: service_healthy zabbix-java-gateway: condition: service_started zabbix-snmptraps: condition: service_started volumes: - ./zabbix_server_data:/var/lib/zabbix - ./zabbix_snmptraps:/var/lib/zabbix/snmptraps - ./zabbix_mibs:/var/lib/zabbix/mibs expose: - 10051 healthcheck: test: - CMD - pgrep - zabbix_server interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_zabbix_server_pgsql' networks: - internal zabbix-web-nginx-pgsql: image: zabbix/zabbix-web-nginx-pgsql:alpine-7.4-latest restart: unless-stopped environment: DB_SERVER_HOST: postgres POSTGRES_USER: {{ postgres_user }} POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ postgres_password }} POSTGRES_DB: {{ postgres_db }} ZBX_SERVER_HOST: zabbix-server-pgsql PHP_TZ: {{ php_tz }} ZBX_SERVER_NAME: {{ zbx_server_name }} depends_on: zabbix-server-pgsql: condition: service_healthy expose: - 8080 healthcheck: test: - CMD - curl - -f - http://localhost:8080/ interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8080' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
